Methodology

Insights

Data stories that explain what South Africa's numbers actually mean — written for everyone, not just economists.

Featured stories

📉UnemploymentFeatured
7 min read

South Africa's Unemployment Crisis

Why one in three South Africans who want work cannot find it — and what is being done about it.

South Africa has one of the world's highest unemployment rates. At 31.4%, more than 7.8 million people are jobless. This story examines the scale of the crisis, its causes, and what progress looks like.

Read story
🛒Inflation & PricesFeatured
5 min read

Inflation: What the Numbers Actually Mean for South Africans

CPI hit a 21-year low in 2025 — but does lower inflation mean life is getting cheaper?

South Africa's annual average inflation hit 3.2% in 2025 — the lowest in 21 years. The headline number suggests relief, but food prices, electricity tariffs, and interest rates still squeeze millions of households.

Read story

More stories

📊GDP & Economy
6 min

SA's Economy: Growing, But Not Fast Enough

South Africa's economy grew 1.1% in 2025 — its strongest performance since 2022. But economists say 5% annual growth is needed to make a real dent in unemployment. This story explains the gap between what is happening and what is needed.

Read
🔍Crime
5 min

Understanding South Africa's Crime Statistics

South Africa recorded 26,232 murders in 2023/24 — a decline from the prior year's peak. But crime statistics are complex documents. This story explains what they measure, what they miss, and how to read them critically.

Read
🎓Education
4 min

Matric 2024: Record Pass Rate, Persistent Inequalities

South Africa's 2024 matric pass rate hit a record 87.3%, up from 82.9% in 2023. That's genuinely good news. But the rate masks large disparities between provinces, school types, and subjects.

Read
SA Data Hub

A modern, accessible platform for exploring South African public data. Built to make government statistics understandable for everyone.

Data sourced from Statistics South Africa, SARB, SAPS and other official bodies. Not affiliated with any government department.
